critic.verdict: A highly comfortable and rhythmically accurate Easy chart that suffers from extreme, boring copy-paste repetition in the second half.

issues.problem: The exact same 6-step pattern (L -> D -> U, R -> D -> HOLD U) is copy-pasted 13 times consecutively. While safe for beginners, this extreme level of repetition fails to reflect any progression or variation in the music's melody and becomes highly monotonous.

critic.verdict: Rhythmically precise and physically comfortable, but severely crippled by extreme, copy-pasted pattern repetition that saps any sense of choreography.

issues.problem: The identical 'L D U R' circular loop is repeated over 12 times consecutively. While playable, it makes the section incredibly monotonous.
The 'U D L R' pattern forces an awkward, repetitive wide-stance body position (stretching Up/Down then Left/Right) with zero variation.
The climax of the song lazily copy-pastes the exact same 'U D L R' 8th-note streams twice, completely ignoring potential visual or melodic variations in the audio.
The outro block defaults back to the same 'L D U R' repetition, concluding the chart with no creative resolution or thematic evolution.

critic.verdict: An energetic and rhythmically stellar chart with excellent drop pacing, held back only by a few awkward crossover flows in the streams and holds.

issues.problem: The 16th-note stream sequence (D -> U -> R -> L -> D -> U -> R -> D) forces uncomfortable double-stepping or an extreme, unplayable body twist.
Tapping L -> R -> D while holding U forces a highly awkward crossover/under-crossover on the free foot.
A JUMP LR is placed during a HOLD U, forcing an unexpected 3-panel bracket/chord that is a bit too technical for a standard Hard rating.
